chapter_title.ar: باب الصَّلَاةِ فِي ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ فِي كَمْ تُصَلِّي وَ صَلَاةِ الْعُرَاةِ وَ التَّوَشُّحِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 59 – The Salāt performed in one piece of cloth, and the woman, how many (clothes) can she pray Salāt in, and a Salāt of the naked, and wearing the scarf

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/301)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/301)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَمَّ قَوْماً فِي قَمِيصٍ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ رِدَاءٌ فَقَالَ لَا يَنْبَغِي إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ عَلَيْهِ رِدَاءٌ أَوْ عِمَامَةٌ يَرْتَدِي بِهَا .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Hisham Bin Salim, from Suleyman Bin Khalid who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about a man who leads a group (in Salāt), being in one shirt, not having a robe upon him. So heasws said: ‘It is not befitting unless there happens to be a cloak upon him, or a turban, wearing with these’.
en.sarwar: 3. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amir Ali ibn Mahziyar from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from Hisham ibn Salim from Sulayman ibn Khalid who has said the following: I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who leads a people in Salah (prayer) wearing a shirt without a gown. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not proper for him to perform Salah (prayer) without a gown or turban to dress up with.’”
